data.title: Mega Merger Alert! Rio Tinto and Glencore Enter Early-Stage Talks to Form a $207B Mining Giant
data.description:
Rio Tinto (RIO) and Glencore (GB:GLEN) have confirmed they are in preliminary discussions about a possible combination of their businesses. Upon completion, the deal would create the world's largest mining company, valued at nearly $207 billion. Both companies noted that the talks are at an early stage and that there is no certainty that an offer will be made.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The talks focus on a possible all-share merger in which Rio Tinto would acquire Glencore through a court-approved scheme of arrangement.

Importantly, the potential merger would bring together two mining giants with complementary strengths. Rio Tinto is known for its world-class iron ore, copper, and aluminum operations, built around long-life, high-quality assets. Meanwhile, Glencore is a major producer of copper, nickel, cobalt, and coal.

The move comes at a time when the mining industry is entering a new wave of consolidation. Companies are racing to lock in key metals for the future, especially copper, as electrification, AI data centers, and rising defense spending push demand to new highs.

Rio Faces Tight Deadline

Rio Tinto now has a clear deadline under the U.K. Takeover Code. By 5:00 p.m. London time on February 5, 2026, it must either declare that it plans to make a formal offer or step back from the talks, unless regulators allow more time.

If it chooses not to move forward, Rule 2.8 would prevent Rio Tinto from making another bid for a set period.

It is worth noting that the two companies held similar talks in early 2025 and in 2014, both of which collapsed due to disagreements over valuation and strategic alignment.

Key Hurdles Ahead 

The deal is expected to face several challenges. Glencore is one of the world's largest coal producers, while Rio Tinto fully exited the coal business in 2018. It is unclear whether Rio would acquire these assets or require Glencore to spin them off first.

Also, Glencore's large commodity trading arm differs from Rio Tinto's traditional asset-heavy operating model, raising questions about cultural and operational integration.

Finally, a merger of this scale would face intense antitrust investigations across multiple jurisdictions, including Australia, the UK, and the EU.

Is RIO a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on RIO stock based on three Buys and three Holds. The average Rio Tinto stock price target of $82.82 indicates 1.62% downside risk.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise ahead of Tariff Ruling and Jobs Report
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended higher on Friday morning as markets awaited a possible U.S. Supreme Court decision on President Donald Trump's tariffs and the jobs report. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.22%, 0.15%, and 0.08%, respectively, at 8:20 a.m. EST on January 9.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major averages closed mixed, as investors pulled back from high-flying tech stocks amid profit-taking and rising concerns over elevated valuations in the AI sector. The Nasdaq Composite dipped 0.44%, primarily weighed down by sharp losses in key chip and software names like Nvidia (NVDA), Palantir (PLTR), and Broadcom (AVGO). Meanwhile, the DJIA climbed 0.55%, buoyed by gains in industrials and financials, and the S&P 500 eked out a negligible gain of less than 0.01%.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of nuclear power companies Vistra (VST) and Oklo (OKLO) rallied 17% and 18%, respectively, after entering into deals to power Meta Platforms' (META) AI goals. Meanwhile, Intel (INTC) stock was up 3% in Friday's pre-market trading after Trump praised the chipmaker on social media following his meeting with the company's CEO Lip-Bu Tan.  

Also, Revolution Medicines (RVMD) stock surged more than 13% after the Financial Times reported a potential acquisition by Merck (MRK).

data.title: Why OKLO Stock Is Soaring 18% Today
data.description:
Shares of U.S.-based Oklo Inc. (OKLO) surged more than 18% in early trading today after Meta Platforms (META) announced deals with three nuclear power providers. As Meta works to secure energy for its growing AI projects, nuclear stocks surged, with Oklo and other energy companies posting strong gains. Vistra Corp. (VST) also rose 17% in early trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Oklo develops compact nuclear microreactors aimed at providing reliable, low-carbon power for commercial and industrial use.

Meta Signs Nuclear Energy Deals

Meta has teamed up with Vistra, TerraPower, and Oklo—all working in nuclear power—to supply energy for its Prometheus supercluster, currently under construction at a data center in New Albany, Ohio. The company said that its collaboration with the three nuclear firms could add 6.6 gigawatts of power by 2035, enough to meet the entire energy demand of New Hampshire.

To be precise, Meta plans to support several small nuclear reactors being developed by Oklo over the next decade.

The deals are the latest step in Meta's push to secure the energy needed for its AI infrastructure as the company works toward Zuckerberg's goal of building superintelligent AI.

What Meta's Deal Means for OKLO

While Meta didn't reveal the contract values, deals of this size could bring billions of dollars in revenue to electricity providers. Being selected by a Big Tech company like Meta also boosts Oklo's credibility, making it easier to attract investors, partners, and customers.

Additionally, the partnership gives Oklo steady demand and support for its small nuclear reactors, which could help speed up construction and production. Meta noted that Oklo's advanced nuclear technology campus in Pike County, Ohio, is expected to be operational by 2030.

Overall, the deal positions Oklo as a leading player in next-generation nuclear energy, potentially opening opportunities for more large-scale energy contracts in the future.

Is OKLO a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on OKLO stock, based on eight Buys and five Holds assigned in the last three months. The average share price target for Oklo is $130.10, which implies an upside of 33.3% from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Amazon Stock (AMZN) Slips on Layoff Expectations
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) stock slipped on Friday alongside reports that the e-commerce company plans to lay off workers soon. The scale of the layoffs is currently unknown, but the company has started its internal review of workers to determine ongoing employment. This shouldn't come as a surprise, as many companies often reduce their workforces at the start of a new year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While some companies have filed WARN notices about their 2026 layoffs, Amazon hasn't done so. That makes sense, as the company typically doesn't file these until after employees have been notified. Amazon is able to do this as it offers severance pay or garden leave to employees, which gives them advance notice or pay. Handling layoffs this way allows Amazon to delay the WARN filing.

According to recent reports, Amazon is expected to lay off anywhere from 1,000 to 2,500 employees, compared to the roughly 1.1 million it employs in the U.S. These layoffs will likely be announced this month, as the company pares down its workforce for 2026.

Amazon Stock Movement Today

Amazon stock was down slightly in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 1.96% rally yesterday. The stock has gained 6.7% year-to-date and 12.49% over the past 12 months.

AMZN stock has experienced muted trading today, as roughly 513,000 shares have changed hands this morning. For the record, the company's three-month daily average trading volume is around 33.39 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: CoreWeave Stock at a Crossroads: Is CRWV Worth $68 — or $130? Analysts Are Split
data.description:
CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) wrapped up its first year as a public company on a sour note. After surging in the months following its high-profile March debut, the AI infrastructure stock has since reversed course, with shares now down about 61% from their June peak.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time, CoreWeave's central role in the AI data-center buildout – along with its close ties to Nvidia – positioned the company as a key beneficiary of the generative AI boom. But that rapid expansion has come at a cost. Heavy borrowing to scale its data-center footprint, persistent net losses, and negative free cash flow are now under greater scrutiny, particularly as talk of an AI bubble grows louder and some on the Street question the company's long-term financial position and path to profitability.

So, should investors stay away from this risky company or load up with the shares at a discount?

If Gil Luria of D.A. Davidson is any guide, the answer sits squarely in between. The analyst has issued what he calls a "reluctant upgrade," moving CoreWeave from Underperform (i.e., Sell) to Neutral, while lifting his price target from $36 to $68. Even so, the new target still sits ~12% below the current share price. (To watch Luria's track record, click here)

So, what is this "reluctant upgrade" all about? Certainly not about an improved long-term opportunity, as Luria bluntly puts it. "We continue to believe that the equity in shares of CRWV may ultimately lose all its value since the entire value of the enterprise is owned by debt holders," the analyst explained. "However, we now see a potential fundraise for OpenAI as a possible short-term catalyst."

Although Luria does not think a successful $100 billion capital raise by OpenAI is a "forgone conclusion," its completion would represent a "potential positive catalyst" for CRWV. A raise would enable OpenAI to meet its 2026 commitments, including those related to CRWV. "While this would not change the value destruction inherent in CRWV's business, it would kick the can down the road for the inevitable reckoning," said Luria on the matter.

By contrast, if OpenAI's fundraising effort falls meaningfully short of the $100 billion target by the end of March, this could accelerate the decline in the shares' value, which is why Luria says he would "continue to steer investors away."

Yet, as is often the case with controversial Wall Street names, Cantor analyst Thomas Blakey has an entirely different take, with the analyst believing the company is "well-positioned to benefit from AI hyperscale demand and more specifically next-generation GPU demand."

Blakey thinks Gemini's late-2025 improvement in relative performance vs. peers, supported by Google's TPUs, created a "buying opportunity" in CRWV stock, with the shares now trading at roughly 3x C27, compared with 6x for ORCL and 9x for MSFT.

"We believe exiting C27 CoreWeave's full contracted 2.9 GW could be sold and nearly activated, creating a forward-looking $30b+ revenue run rate opportunity for C28, or an over 80% C25-C28 revenue CAGR," the analyst explained.

And that, says Blakey, justifies his $131 price target, which points toward 12-month returns of a hefty 70%. Naturally, Blakey rates the shares as Overweight (i.e., Buy). (To watch Blakey's track record, click here)

Overall, Wall Street reflects a mix of optimism and caution, with 13 Buys alongside 12 Holds – enough to earn the stock a Moderate Buy consensus rating. In aggregate, the Street sees meaningful upside ahead, with the average price target of $126.43 pointing to 64% gains over the coming year. (See CRWV stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Andreessen Horowitz Rips Through Funding Records with a Massive $15 Billion AI Bet
data.description:
The kings of Sand Hill Road are officially armed for a new era of technological warfare. On Friday, Andreessen Horowitz announced it has successfully raised over $15 billion, more than doubling its previous fundraising effort from 2024. This massive influx of cash pushes the firm's total assets under management past the $90 billion mark. The firm's leadership made it clear that is about ensuring that the United States maintains its lead in the global struggle for tech supremacy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Funds Will Be Used to Back More Mature Startups

Nearly half of this record-breaking money is going straight into a growth fund. This specific pile of cash will help bigger, successful startups like the defense company Anduril and the software firm Databricks. Driving this move is a major focus on projects that strengthen the country, making the firm a key player in national security. The money for defense and national interest tech has almost doubled to $1.2 billion, showing a clear shift toward building tools that protect the U.S.

Artificial Intelligence Powers the Venture Portfolio

While defense is a major focus, the firm is pouring an unprecedented amount of capital into the AI revolution. The application and infrastructure funds, which act as the backbone for AI startups, were boosted to $1.7 billion each. This is a significant jump from previous years and proves that the firm believes the AI boom is nowhere near its peak. Co-founder Ben Horowitz noted that the fate of new technology in the U.S. rests partly on the firm's shoulders, especially as American companies face intense competition from rivals in China.

Lawmakers and Tech Giants Shape the 2026 Landscape

This massive raise comes as the firm becomes increasingly active in the political arena. Andreessen Horowitz has already joined a $100 million network aimed at influencing how AI is regulated in Washington.

At the heart of this strategy lies a determination to shape the rules of the game while co-founder Marc Andreessen emerges as a high-profile supporter of the current administration. Despite some cooling in the biotech and gaming sectors, the firm's overall message remains aggressive. They are betting billions that the next century of tech will be won or lost on American soil.

Investors interested in insider profiles can get information on various funds by Andreessen Horowitz on TipRanks. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Cathie Wood Buys the Dip in Broadcom Stock (AVGO) as Wall Street Grows More Bullish
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) stock has risen 48% in 2005, driven by strong AI demand for its custom ASIC chips and networking products. The stock has pulled back in recent weeks, as investors question the returns from heavy AI spending by big tech firms and worry about rich valuations. Still, some buyers are stepping in. Cathie Wood bought the dip in Broadcom stock by purchasing 31,573 shares via the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) on January 8. More broadly, Wall Street remains bullish on AVGO stock and sees solid upside potential in 2026, supported by steady demand for Broadcom's AI chips and networking gear.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Top Analysts Are Upbeat About Broadcom Stock

Today, Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon maintained an Outperform rating on Broadcom with a $475 price target. He said fears about new rivals and customer-built chips are overstated after a long meeting with management. Rasgon pointed to Broadcom's scale and strong supply chain in custom AI chips. He also noted that while Nvidia (NVDA) sets the pace in AI innovation, Broadcom is uniquely positioned to help customers keep up with that roadmap.

Earlier this week, Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider added Broadcom to the firm's U.S. Conviction List. He said Broadcom's strong position in enterprise networking chips should help it gain share in custom processors used by major U.S. cloud firms. Goldman kept a Buy rating on the stock with a $450 price target, implying 35% upside from current levels.

Likewise, JPMorgan Chase has picked Broadcom as its top name in the chip sector. 5-star analyst Harlan Sur  expects AI-related revenue to reach $55 billion to $60 billion in fiscal 2026 and keeps a Buy rating with a $475 price target. He said data center spending is still rising and could grow sharply again next year, which should support chipmakers across the supply chain, including networking and memory firms.

Is AVGO Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

With 27 Buys and two Holds, Broadcom stock scores a Strong Buy consensus rating. The average AVGO stock price target of $461.42 indicates 38.78% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Analysts Turn Bullish as Bloom Energy's (BE) $2.65B AEP Deal Signals Surging Demand
data.description:
Bloom Energy's (BE) massive $2.65 billion fuel‑cell purchase deal with American Electric Power (AEP) has drawn bullish analyst reactions. The deal has raised optimism about the company's long‑term growth prospects and the scale of demand for its solid oxide technology.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Clear Street: Sales Should "Benefit Strongly" From Wyoming Project

Clear Street analyst Tim Moore CFA has raised the price target on Bloom Energy stock to $68 from $58, citing a stronger sales outlook.

While the analyst maintains a Hold rating, he emphasized that Bloom is expected to benefit from a recent order of solid oxide fuel cells tied to the Wyoming facility.

Moore estimates the Wyoming project could approach $3 billion in total costs, including construction and non‑fuel‑cell infrastructure. While not all of that revenue flows to Bloom, the analyst sees the scale of the project as a major validation of Bloom's technology and commercial traction.

 RBC: The $2.65B Purchase Could Be Even Bigger Than Expected

RBC Capital's Chris Dendrinos highlighted that AEP's unconditional purchase agreement covers a "substantial portion" of its optioned fuel‑cell capacity, a move that could represent a major acceleration of Bloom's deployment pipeline.

The analyst is only uncertain about one question: is this project connected to the BFC Power/Crusoe 900 MW project in Cheyenne, or is it separate? If it's a standalone project, Dendrinos thinks investors will view the $2.65 billion deal even more positively because it would signal that several large fuel‑cell projects are moving forward at the same time.

RBC reiterated that AEP had already secured 100 MW from Bloom in 2024, with an option for an additional 900 MW, and this latest move suggests that option is now being exercised at scale.

Evercore ISI: A "Meaningful Positive" That Signals Stronger-Than-Expected Demand

Evercore ISI analyst Nicholas Amicucci maintained a Buy rating and a $152 price target, calling it one of the firm's top ideas thanks to its fast-to-deploy, reliable fuel‑cell power.

He sees Bloom well positioned for rising AI‑driven electricity needs, especially as smaller data centers require quiet, permit‑light, local power sources that play to Bloom's strengths.

Is BE Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Bloom Energy stock based on nine Buys, eight Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average BE stock price target of $121.59 indicates 0.21% downside risk.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
